name: Neti
description: Neti is the gatekeeper of the Underworld (Kur) in Sumerian mythology. He is a minor deity, but his role is crucial as he guards the seven gates that lead to the realm of Ereshkigal, the queen of the Underworld. Neti's primary function is to control access to Kur, ensuring that only those who are permitted may enter. He is responsible for announcing the arrival of visitors and relaying messages between them and Ereshkigal. He demands obeisance and respect from those who seek to pass through the gates, acting as a symbolic representation of the passage from the world of the living to the land of the dead. Neti is not typically depicted with specific powers beyond his authority over the gates, but his steadfastness and unwavering adherence to Ereshkigal's commands make him a formidable figure. His origins are not explicitly detailed in surviving texts, but his existence is integral to the narrative structure of the Underworld and its interactions with the world of the living. He is a necessary component in the myths involving descents into the Underworld, such as Inanna's journey. He represents the boundary between life and death, and the inevitable transition that all must face. His stern nature and unwavering loyalty to Ereshkigal reinforce the Underworld's reputation as a place of no return.
subtitle: Gatekeeper of the Sumerian Underworld

keyMyths.title: Inanna's Descent
keyMyths.description: Neti challenges Inanna at each of the seven gates, demanding she remove an item of clothing or jewelry before proceeding. This symbolizes the stripping away of earthly power and identity before entering the Underworld.

keyMyths.title: Ereshkigal's Commands
keyMyths.description: Neti follows Ereshkigal's orders without question, highlighting his loyalty and the strict rules governing the Underworld. He is a symbol of the unyielding nature of death.

keyMyths.title: Passage to the Underworld
keyMyths.description: Neti controls the passage of spirits into the Underworld, ensuring that only those who have properly died and been mourned may enter. He upholds the laws of the afterlife.

sources.author: Samuel Noah Kramer
sources.work: The Sumerians: Their History, Culture, and Character
sources.description: Provides a comprehensive overview of Sumerian mythology and religion.
sources.text: Provides a comprehensive overview of Sumerian mythology and religion.

sources.author: Diane Wolkstein and Samuel Noah Kramer
sources.work: Inanna: Queen of Heaven and Earth
sources.description: Offers a detailed translation and analysis of the myth of Inanna's descent.
sources.text: Offers a detailed translation and analysis of the myth of Inanna's descent.
